AdaptationPolicy_2.name: AdaptationPolicy_2
AdaptationPolicy_2.brief: The concept AdaptationPolicy_2 defines the requirements on the predicate functors that determine whether a feature of the triangulated Delaunay graph should be rejected or not. It also provides a functor for inserting sites in the Delaunay graph. The last functor is optional and a tag determines whether it is provided or not. Note that while the first two functors do not modify the Delaunay graph they take as an argument, the last ones does.
AdaptationPolicy_2.detailed: Refines CopyConstructible Assignable DefaultConstructible Has models CGAL::Identity_policy_2 <DG,AT> CGAL::Apollonius_graph_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <AG2> CGAL::Apollonius_graph_caching_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <AG2> CGAL::Delaunay_triangulation_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <DT2> CGAL::Delaunay_triangulation_caching_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <DT2> CGAL::Regular_triangulation_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <RT2> CGAL::Regular_triangulation_caching_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <RT2> CGAL::Segment_Delaunay_graph_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <SDG2> CGAL::Segment_Delaunay_graph_caching_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <SDG2> DelaunayGraph_2 AdaptationTraits_2 CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2 <DG,AT,AP>

members.name: Site_2
members.brief: A type for the sites of the Voronoi diagram.

members.name: Delaunay_graph
members.brief: A type for the triangulated Delaunay graph.
members.detailed: The type Delaunay_graph must be a model of the DelaunayGraph_2 concept.

members.name: Edge_rejector
members.brief: A type for the predicate functor that is responsible for rejecting an edge of the Delaunay graph (or equivalently rejecting its dual edge in the Voronoi diagram).
members.detailed: It must be model of the concepts DefaultConstructible , CopyConstructible , Assignable , and AdaptableBinaryFunction . It must provide the following operators: bool operator()(Delaunay_graph dg, Delaunay_edge e) bool operator()(Delaunay_graph dg, Delaunay_face_handle f, int i) bool operator()(Delaunay_graph dg, Delaunay_edge_circulator ec) bool operator()(Delaunay_graph dg, All_Delaunay_edges_iterator eit) bool operator()(Delaunay_graph dg, Finite_Delaunay_edges_iterator eit) The functor returns true iff the edge is rejected.

members.name: Face_rejector
members.brief: A type for the predicate functor that is responsible for rejecting a vertex of the Delaunay graph (or equivalently its dual face in the Voronoi diagram - hence the name of the functor).
members.detailed: It must be model of the concepts DefaultConstructible , CopyConstructible , Assignable , AdaptableBinaryFunction . It must provide the following operator: bool operator()(Delaunay graph dg, Delaunay_vertex_handle v) The functor returns true iff the face is rejected.

members.name: Has_inserter
members.brief: A tag for determining if the adaptation policy class provides a functor for inserting sites in the Delaunay graph.
members.detailed: This tag is equal to either CGAL::Tag_true (a site inserter functor is available) or CGAL::Tag_false (a site inserter functor is not available).

members.name: Site_inserter
members.brief: A type for a functor that inserts sites in the Delaunay graph.
members.detailed: It must be model of the concepts DefaultConstructible , CopyConstructible , Assignable , AdaptableBinaryFunction following operator Delaunay_vertex_handle operator()( Delaunay_graph & dg, Site_2 t) The vertex handle returned either points to the vertex of the Delaunay graph corresponding to the site just inserted or is the default constructed vertex handle. The latter case can happen if the site inserted is hidden , i.e., it has an empty Voronoi cell. This type is required only if the Has_inserter tag is equal to CGAL::Tag_true .

members.name: site_inserter_object
members.brief: This method is required only if Has_inserter is equal to CGAL::Tag_true .

members.name: clear
members.brief: Clears the state of the adaptation policy.

members.name: swap
members.brief: The adaptation policies ap and other are swapped.
members.detailed: This method should be preferred to ap=other or ap(other) if other is deleted afterwards.

members.name: is_valid
members.brief: Tests the validity of the adaptation policy.

members.name: is_valid
members.brief: Tests the validity of the adaptation policy using extra information from the Delaunay graph dg .

AdaptationTraits_2.name: AdaptationTraits_2
AdaptationTraits_2.brief: The concept AdaptationTraits_2 defines the functors required for accessing geometric information in the Delaunay graph that is needed by the CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2 <DG,AT,AP> class. It optionally defines a functor for performing nearest site queries. A tag is provided for determining whether this functor is defined or not.
AdaptationTraits_2.detailed: Refines CopyConstructible Assignable DefaultConstructible Has models CGAL::Apollonius_graph_adaptation_traits_2 <AG2> CGAL::Delaunay_triangulation_adaptation_traits_2 <DT2> CGAL::Regular_triangulation_adaptation_traits_2 <RT2> CGAL::Segment_Delaunay_graph_adaptation_traits_2 <SDG2> DelaunayGraph_2 CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2 <DG,AT,AP>

members.name: Point_2
members.brief: A type for a point.

members.name: Site_2
members.brief: A type for the sites of the Voronoi diagram.

members.name: Delaunay_graph
members.brief: A type for the triangulated Delaunay graph.
members.detailed: The type Delaunay_graph must be a model of the DelaunayGraph_2 concept.

members.name: Delaunay_edge
members.brief: The type of the edges of the Delaunay graph.

members.name: Delaunay_face_handle
members.brief: The type of the face handles of the Delaunay graph.

members.name: Delaunay_vertex_handle
members.brief: The type of the vertex handles of the Delaunay graph.

members.name: Access_site_2
members.brief: A type for a functor that accesses the site associated with a vertex.
members.detailed: The functor should be a model of the concepts DefaultConstructible , CopyConstructible , Assignable and AdaptableUnaryFunction . The functor must provide the following operator: result_type operator()(Delaunay_vertex_handle v) where the result type result_type must be either Site_2 or const Site_2 & .

members.name: Construct_Voronoi_point_2
members.brief: A type for a functor that constructs the dual point of a (triangular) face in the Delaunay graph.
members.detailed: This point is the Voronoi vertex of the three sites defining the face in the Delaunay graph. The functor must be a model of the concepts DefaultConstructible , CopyConstructible , Assignable , AdaptableUnaryFunction . It must provide the following operator: Point_2 operator()(Delaunay_face_handle f) . The face handle f must not correspond to an infinite face.

members.name: Has_nearest_site_2
members.brief: A tag for determining if the adaptation traits class provides a functor for performing nearest site queries.
members.detailed: This tag is equal to either CGAL::Tag_true (a nearest site query functor is available) or CGAL::Tag_false (a nearest site query functor is not available).

members.name: Nearest_site_2
members.brief: A type for a functor that performs nearest site queries.
members.detailed: Semantically, the result of the query is either a face, edge or vertex of the Delaunay graph. It is a face if the query point has at least three closest sites; the returned face has closest sites as vertices. It is an edge if the query point is equidistant to exactly two vertices of the Delaunay graph, which are the source and target vertices of the edge. In all other cases, the search result is a vertex, namely, the unique vertex of the Delaunay graph closest to the query point. The functor must be a model of the concepts DefaultConstructible , CopyConstructible , Assignable , AdaptableBinaryFunction . It must provide the following operator: result_type operator()(Delaunay_graph dg, Point_2 p) where the result type result_type is std::variant< Delaunay_vertex_handle , Delaunay_edge , Delaunay_face_handle > . This type is required only if Has_nearest_site_2 is equal to CGAL::Tag_true .

members.name: nearest_site_2_object
members.brief: This method is required only if Has_nearest_site_2 is equal to CGAL::Tag_true .

CGAL::Apollonius_graph_adaptation_traits.name: CGAL::Apollonius_graph_adaptation_traits_2

CGAL::Apollonius_graph_adaptation_traits.detailed: The template parameter of the Apollonius_graph_adaptation_traits_2 class must be a model of the DelaunayGraph_2 concept, and in particular it has the semantics of a (triangulated) 2D Apollonius graph. Is model of AdaptationTraits_2 AdaptationTraits_2 DelaunayGraph_2 CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2 <DG,AT,AP> CGAL::Apollonius_graph_2<Gt,Agds> CGAL::Apollonius_graph_hierarchy_2<Gt,Agds>

CGAL::Apollonius_graph_caching_degenerac.name: CGAL::Apollonius_graph_caching_degeneracy_removal_policy_2
CGAL::Apollonius_graph_caching_degenerac.brief: The class Apollonius_graph_caching_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 provides a model for the AdaptationPolicy_2 concept.
CGAL::Apollonius_graph_caching_degenerac.detailed: The template parameter of the Apollonius_graph_caching_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 class must be a model of the DelaunayGraph_2 concept, and in particular it has the semantics of a (triangulated) 2D Apollonius graph. This policy caches the results of the edge and face rejectors and results in a Voronoi diagram that has no degenerate features, i.e., no Voronoi edges of zero length and no Voronoi faces of zero area. CGAL::Graphics_scene_options_voronoi_dia.detailed: This class is a model of GraphicsSceneOptionsVoronoiDiagram2 . DS a CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2 . VertexDescriptor a descriptor of vertices of DS . EdgeDescriptor a descriptor of edges of DS . FaceDescriptor a descriptor of faces of DS . Is model of GraphicsSceneOptionsVoronoiDiagram2

CGAL::Identity_policy_2.name: CGAL::Identity_policy_2
CGAL::Identity_policy_2.brief: The class Identity_policy_2 provides a model for the AdaptationPolicy_2 concept.
CGAL::Identity_policy_2.detailed: The first template parameter of the Identity_policy_2 class must be a model of the DelaunayGraph_2 concept, whereas as the second template parameter must be a model of the AdaptationTraits_2 concept. This policy rejects no edge and no face of the Delaunay graph, thus giving a Voronoi diagram which is the true dual of the triangulation Delaunay graph. The Voronoi diagram created with this adaptation policy may have degenerate features, such as Voronoi edges of zero length, or Voronoi faces of zero area. This policy assumes that the Delaunay graph, that is adapted, allows for site insertions through an insert method that takes as argument an object of type AT::Site_2 . The site inserter functor provided by this policy uses the aforementioned insert method. Is model of AdaptationPolicy_2 AdaptationTraits_2 DelaunayGraph_2 CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2 <DG,AT,AP>

Is model of AdaptationTraits_2 AdaptationTraits_2 DelaunayGraph_2 Voronoi_diagram_2 <DG,AT,AP> CGAL::Regular_triangulation_2<Traits,Tds>

CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2.name: CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2
CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2.brief: The class Voronoi_diagram_2 provides an adaptor that enables us to view a triangulated Delaunay graph as their dual subdivision, the Voronoi diagram.
CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2.detailed: The class Voronoi_diagram_2 is designed to provide an API that is similar to that of CGAL's arrangements. DG corresponds to the triangulated Delaunay graph and must be a model of the DelaunayGraph_2 concept. AT must be a model of the AdaptationTraits_2 concept. AP must be a model of the AdaptationPolicy_2 concept. The third template parameter defaults to CGAL::Identity_policy_2 <DG,AT> . Refines CopyConstructible Assignable DefaultConstructible CGAL::Delaunay_triangulation_2<Traits,Tds> CGAL::Regular_triangulation_2<Traits,Tds> CGAL::Triangulation_hierarchy_2<Tr> provided that Tr is a model of DelaunayGraph_2 CGAL::Segment_Delaunay_graph_2<Gt,DS> CGAL::Segment_Delaunay_graph_hierarchy_2<Gt,STag,DS> CGAL::Apollonius_graph_2<Gt,Agds> CGAL::Apollonius_graph_hierarchy_2<Gt,Agds>

members.definition: typedef unspecified_type CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2< DG, AT, AP >::Bounded_faces_iterator

members.brief: A type for an iterator over the unbounded halfedges of the Voronoi diagram.
members.detailed: Its value type is Halfedge .
members.definition: typedef unspecified_type CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2< DG, AT, AP >::Unbounded_halfedges_iterator

members.brief: A type for an iterator over the bounded halfedges of the Voronoi diagram.
members.detailed: Its value type is Halfedge .

members.brief: A type for an iterator over the sites of the Voronoi diagram.
members.detailed: Its value type is Site_2 .
members.definition: typedef unspecified_type CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2< DG, AT, AP >::Site_iterator

members.name: Locate_result
members.brief: The result type of the point location queries.

members.name: Voronoi_diagram_2
members.brief: Creates a Voronoi diagram using at as adaptation traits and ap as adaptation policy; the underlying Delaunay graph is created using gt as geometric traits.

members.name: Voronoi_diagram_2
members.brief: Creates a Voronoi diagram from the Delaunay graph dg and using at as adaptation traits and ap as adaptation policy.
members.detailed: The Delaunay graph dg is fully copied if swap_dg is set to false , or swapped with the one stored internally if swap_dg is set to true .
members.argsstring: (Delaunay_graph dg, bool swap_dg=false, Adaptation_traits at=Adaptation_traits(), Adaptation_policy ap=Adaptation_policy())

members.name: Voronoi_diagram_2
members.brief: Creates a Voronoi diagram using as sites the sites in the iterator range [first, beyond) , at as adaptation traits and ap as adaptation policy; the underlying Delaunay graph is created using gt as geometric traits.
members.detailed: Iterator must be a model of the InputIterator concept and its value type must be Site_2 .
members.argsstring: (Iterator first, Iterator beyond, Adaptation_traits at=Adaptation_traits(), Adaptation_policy ap=Adaptation_policy(), Delaunay_geom_traits gt=Delaunay_geom_traits())

members.name: dual
members.brief: Returns a const reference to the dual graph, i.e., the Delaunay graph.

members.name: dual
members.brief: Returns a handle to the halfedge in the Voronoi diagram that is dual to the edge e in the Delaunay graph.

members.name: dual
members.brief: Returns a handle to the face in the Voronoi diagram that is dual to the vertex corresponding to the vertex handle v in the Delaunay graph.

members.name: dual
members.brief: Returns a handle to the vertex in the Voronoi diagram that is dual to the face corresponding to the face handle f in the Delaunay graph.

members.name: adaptation_traits
members.brief: Returns a reference to the Voronoi traits.

members.name: adaptation_policy
members.brief: Returns a reference to the adaptation policy.

members.name: number_of_vertices
members.brief: Returns the number of Voronoi vertices.

members.name: number_of_faces
members.brief: Returns the number of Voronoi faces (bounded and unbounded).

members.name: number_of_halfedges
members.brief: Returns the number of halfedges (bounded and unbounded) in the Voronoi diagram.
members.detailed: This is always an even number.

members.name: number_of_connected_components
members.brief: Returns the number of connected components of the Voronoi skeleton.

members.name: unbounded_face
members.brief: Returns one of the unbounded faces of the Voronoi diagram.
members.detailed: If no unbounded faces exist (this can happen if the number of sites is zero) the default constructed face handle is returned.

members.name: bounded_face
members.brief: Returns one of the bounded faces of the Voronoi diagram.
members.detailed: If no bounded faces exist the default constructed face handle is returned.

members.name: unbounded_halfedge
members.brief: Returns one of the unbounded halfedges of the Voronoi diagram.
members.detailed: If no unbounded halfedges exist the default constructed halfedge handle is returned.

members.name: bounded_halfedge
members.brief: Returns one of the bounded halfedges of the Voronoi diagram.
members.detailed: If no bounded halfedges exist the default constructed halfedge handle is returned.

members.name: faces_begin
members.brief: Starts at an arbitrary Voronoi face.

members.name: faces_end
members.brief: Past-the-end iterator.

members.name: unbounded_faces_begin
members.brief: Starts at an arbitrary unbounded Voronoi face.

members.name: unbounded_faces_end
members.brief: Past-the-end iterator.

members.name: bounded_faces_begin
members.brief: Starts at an arbitrary bounded Voronoi face.

members.name: bounded_faces_end
members.brief: Past-the-end iterator.

members.name: edges_begin
members.brief: Starts at an arbitrary Voronoi edge.

members.name: edges_end
members.brief: Past-the-end iterator.

members.name: halfedges_begin
members.brief: Starts at an arbitrary Voronoi halfedge.

members.name: halfedges_end
members.brief: Past-the-end iterator.

members.name: unbounded_halfedges_begin
members.brief: Starts at an arbitrary unbounded Voronoi edge.

members.name: unbounded_halfedges_end
members.brief: Past-the-end iterator.

members.name: bounded_halfedges_begin
members.brief: Starts at an arbitrary bounded Voronoi edge.

members.name: bounded_halfedges_end
members.brief: Past-the-end iterator.

members.name: vertices_begin
members.brief: Starts at an arbitrary Voronoi vertex.

members.name: vertices_end
members.brief: Past-the-end iterator.

members.name: sites_begin
members.brief: Starts at an arbitrary site.

members.name: sites_end
members.brief: Past-the-end iterator.

members.name: ccb_halfedges
members.brief: Returns a circulator over the halfedges on the boundary of f .
members.detailed: The circulator is initialized to an arbitrary halfedge on the boundary of the Voronoi face f .

members.name: ccb_halfedges
members.brief: Returns a circulator over the halfedges on the boundary of f .
members.detailed: The circulator is initialized with the halfedge h . The halfedge h must lie on the boundary of f .

members.name: incident_halfedges
members.brief: Returns a circulator over the halfedges whose target is the Voronoi vertex v .
members.detailed: The circulator is initialized to an arbitrary halfedge incident to v .

members.name: incident_halfedges
members.brief: Returns a circulator over the halfedges whose target is the Voronoi vertex v .
members.detailed: The circulator is initialized with the halfedge h . The vertex v must be the target vertex of the halfedge h .

members.name: insert
members.brief: Inserts the site t in the Voronoi diagram.
members.detailed: A handle to the face corresponding to the Voronoi face of t in the Voronoi diagram is returned. If t has an empty Voronoi cell, the default constructed face handle is returned. This method is supported only if Voronoi_traits::Has_inserter is set to CGAL::Tag_true .

members.name: insert
members.brief: Inserts, in the Voronoi diagram, the sites in the iterator range [first, beyond) .
members.detailed: The value type of Iterator must be Site_2 . The number of sites in the iterator range is returned. This method is supported only if Voronoi_traits::Has_inserter is set to CGAL::Tag_true .

members.name: locate
members.brief: Performs point location for the query point p .
members.detailed: In other words, the face, halfedge or vertex of the Voronoi diagram is found on which the point p lies. This method is supported only if Voronoi_traits::Has_nearest_site_2 is set to CGAL::Tag_true . The Voronoi diagram must contain at least one face.

members.name: file_output
members.brief: Writes the current state of the Voronoi diagram to the output stream os .
members.detailed: The following operator must be defined: std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream&, Delaunay_graph )

members.name: file_input
members.brief: Reads the current state of the Voronoi diagram from the input stream is .
members.detailed: The following operator must be defined: std::istream& operator>>(std::istream&, Delaunay_graph )

members.name: operator<<
members.brief: Writes the current state of the Voronoi diagram to the output stream os .
members.detailed: The following operator must be defined: std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream&, Delaunay_graph )

members.name: operator>>
members.brief: Reads the current state of the Voronoi diagram from the input stream is .
members.detailed: The following operator must be defined: std::istream& operator>>(std::istream&, Delaunay_graph )

members.name: is_valid
members.brief: Checks the validity of the dual Delaunay graph and the Voronoi diagram adaptor.

members.name: clear
members.brief: Clears all contents of the Voronoi diagram.

members.name: swap
members.brief: The Voronoi diagrams other and vd are swapped.
members.detailed: vd . swap(other) should be preferred to vd``= other or to vd``(other) if other is deleted afterwards.

members.name: halfedge
members.brief: Returns an incident halfedge on the boundary of f .

members.name: ccb
members.brief: Returns a bidirectional circulator for traversing the halfedges on the boundary of f .
members.detailed: The halfedges are traversed in counterclockwise order.

members.name: dual
members.brief: Returns a handle to the corresponding dual vertex in the Delaunay graph.

members.name: is_unbounded
members.brief: Returns true iff the face is an unbounded face in the Voronoi diagram.

members.name: is_halfedge_on_ccb
members.brief: Returns true iff e is a halfedge of the boundary of f .

members.name: is_valid
members.brief: Returns true iff the following conditions are met: the face is not rejected by the chosen adaptation policy; all its adjacent halfedges do not have zero length; all its adjacent halfedges return the face as their adjacent face.

members.name: twin
members.brief: Returns the twin halfedge.

members.name: opposite
members.brief: Same as e.twin() .

members.name: next
members.brief: Returns the next halfedge in the counterclockwise sense around the boundary of the face that e is incident to.

members.name: previous
members.brief: Returns the previous halfedge in the counterclockwise sense around the boundary of the adjacent face.

members.name: face
members.brief: Returns the face that e is incident to.

members.name: source
members.brief: Returns the source vertex of e .
members.detailed: The source vertex must exist, i.e., has_source() must return true .

members.name: target
members.brief: Returns the target vertex of e .
members.detailed: The target vertex must exist, i.e., has_target() must return true .

members.name: ccb
members.brief: Returns a bidirectional circulator to traverse the halfedges on the boundary of the Voronoi face containing e .
members.detailed: The circulator is initialized to e . Applying operator++ (resp. operator- ) to this circulator returns the next halfedge on the boundary of the face containing e in the counterclockwise (resp. clockwise) sense.

members.name: dual
members.brief: Returns the corresponding dual edge in the Delaunay graph.

members.name: up
members.brief: Returns a handle to the vertex in the Delaunay graph corresponding to the defining site above the Voronoi edge.

members.name: down
members.brief: Returns a handle to the vertex in the Delaunay graph corresponding to the defining site below the Voronoi edge.

members.name: left
members.brief: Returns a handle to the vertex in the Delaunay graph corresponding to the defining site to the left of the Voronoi edge.
members.detailed: has_source() must be true .

members.name: right
members.brief: Returns a handle to the vertex in the Delaunay graph corresponding to the defining site to the right of the Voronoi edge.
members.detailed: has_target() must be true .

members.name: has_source
members.brief: Returns true iff the halfedge corresponds to a bisecting segment or a bisecting ray oriented appropriately so that its apex is its source.

members.name: has_target
members.brief: Returns true iff the halfedge corresponds to a bisecting segment or a bisecting ray oriented appropriately so that its apex is its target.

members.name: is_unbounded
members.brief: Returns true iff the source or the target of the halfedge does not exist, i.e., if either of has_source() or has_target() return false .

members.name: is_bisector
members.brief: Returns true iff the Voronoi edge is an entire bisector.

members.name: is_segment
members.brief: Returns true iff the Voronoi edge has both a source and a target Voronoi vertex.

members.name: is_ray
members.brief: Returns true iff the Voronoi edge has either a source or a target Voronoi vertex, but not both; in other words it is a bisecting ray.

members.name: is_valid
members.brief: Returns true if the following conditions are met: the halfedge is not a rejected edge with respect to the chosen adaptation policy; the twin edge of its twin edge is itself; its adjacent face is not a rejected face with respect to the chosen adaptation policy; its source and target vertices are valid (provided they exist, of course); the previous of its next halfedge is itself and the next of its previous halfedge is itself.

members.name: halfedge
members.brief: Returns an incident halfedge that has v as its target.

members.name: degree
members.brief: Returns the in-degree of the vertex, i.e. the number of halfedges that have v as their target.

members.name: point
members.brief: Returns the point represented by the vertex.

members.name: dual
members.brief: Returns a handle to the corresponding dual face in the Delaunay graph.

members.name: site
members.brief: Returns a handle to the vertex in the Delaunay graph corresponding to the $ (i+1)$ -th generating site of the Voronoi vertex.
members.detailed: i must be smaller or equal to 2.

members.name: incident_halfedges
members.brief: Returns a bidirectional circulator that allows the traversal of the halfedges that have v as their target.
members.detailed: Applying operator++ (resp. operator- ) to this circulator returns the next incident halfedge in the counterclockwise (resp. clockwise) sense.

members.name: is_incident_edge
members.brief: Returns true if the halfedge e is incident to v .

members.name: is_incident_face
members.brief: Returns true if the face f is incident to v .

members.name: is_valid
members.brief: Returns true if the following conditions are met: the dual face is not an infinite face; all incident halfedges have the vertex as their target.

DelaunayGraph_2.name: DelaunayGraph_2
DelaunayGraph_2.brief: The concept DelaunayGraph_2 defines the requirements for the first template parameter of the Voronoi_diagram_2<DG,AT,AP> class. The DelaunayGraph_2 concept essentially defines the requirements that a class representing a Delaunay graph must obey so that the Voronoi diagram adaptor can adapt it.
DelaunayGraph_2.detailed: Refines CopyConstructible Assignable DefaultConstructible Traversal of the Delaunay graph A model of the DelaunayGraph_2 concept must provide several iterators and circulators that allow to traverse it (completely or partially). All iterators and circulators must be convertible to the corresponding handles. Has models CGAL::Triangulation_hierarchy_2<Tr> provided that Tr is a model of DelaunayGraph_2 CGAL::Delaunay_triangulation_2<Traits,Tds> CGAL::Regular_triangulation_2<Traits,Tds> CGAL::Segment_Delaunay_graph_2<Gt,DS> CGAL::Segment_Delaunay_graph_hierarchy_2<Gt,STag,DS> CGAL::Apollonius_graph_2<Gt,Agds> CGAL::Apollonius_graph_hierarchy_2<Gt,Agds> AdaptationTraits_2 AdaptationPolicy_2 CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2 <DG,AT,AP>

members.detailed: It must be a model of the concept TriangulationDataStructure_2.

GraphicsSceneOptionsVoronoiDiagram2.name: GraphicsSceneOptionsVoronoiDiagram2
GraphicsSceneOptionsVoronoiDiagram2.brief: The concept GraphicsSceneOptionsVoronoiDiagram2 defines data and methods used to tune the way that the cells of a Voronoi_diagram_2 are considered for drawing or to be added into a graphics scene.
GraphicsSceneOptionsVoronoiDiagram2.detailed: Refines GraphicsSceneOptions Has models CGAL::Graphics_scene_options_voronoi_diagram_2<DS, VertexDescriptor, EdgeDescriptor, FaceDescriptor>

PkgVoronoiDiagram2Ref.name: PkgVoronoiDiagram2Ref
PkgVoronoiDiagram2Ref.detailed: Menelaos Karavelas The 2D Voronoi diagram adaptor package provides an adaptor that adapts a 2-dimensional triangulated Delaunay graph to the corresponding Voronoi diagram, represented as a doubly connected edge list (DCEL) data structure. The adaptor has the ability to automatically eliminate, in a consistent manner, degenerate features of the Voronoi diagram, that are artifacts of the requirement that Delaunay graphs should be triangulated even in degenerate configurations. Depending on the type of operations that the underlying Delaunay graph supports, the adaptor allows for the incremental or dynamic construction of Voronoi diagrams and can support point location queries. Introduced in: CGAL 3.2 BibTeX: cgal:k-vda2-26a License: GPL Windows Demos: 2D Point Voronoi Diagram , 2D Disk Voronoi Diagram , 2D Segment Voronoi Diagram CGAL provides the class CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2 <DG,AT,AP> for adapting the various (triangulated) Delaunay graphs to Voronoi diagrams according to some adaptation policy. In particular, the class CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2 <DG,AT,AP> provides an API for the duals of (triangulated) Delaunay graphs, that makes them look like planar subdivisions. The adaptation policy is responsible for deciding which edges and faces of these duals should be eliminated. This is especially important when, for instance, we want to eliminate degenerate features in the Voronoi diagram that are the result of the fact that Delaunay graphs are always triangulated and are due to degenerate configurations in the generating data. The three template parameters must be models of the DelaunayGraph_2 , AdaptationTraits_2 and AdaptationPolicy_2 concepts, respectively. The first concept is related to the Delaunay graphs that are to be adapted, whereas the second one is responsible for manipulating/accessing in a unified way the geometry of a specific Voronoi diagram as well as for performing nearest site queries. The third template parameter corresponds to the chosen adaptation policy and provides the necessary types and functors needed for performing this adaptation. Classified Reference Pages Concepts DelaunayGraph_2 AdaptationTraits_2 AdaptationPolicy_2 Classes CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2 <DG,AT,AP> CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2<DG,AT,AP>::Halfedge CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2<DG,AT,AP>::Vertex CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2<DG,AT,AP>::Face CGAL::Apollonius_graph_adaptation_traits_2 <AG2> CGAL::Delaunay_triangulation_adaptation_traits_2 <DT2> CGAL::Regular_triangulation_adaptation_traits_2 <RT2> CGAL::Segment_Delaunay_graph_adaptation_traits_2 <SDG2> CGAL::Identity_policy_2 <DG,AT> CGAL::Apollonius_graph_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <AG2> CGAL::Apollonius_graph_caching_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <AG2> CGAL::Delaunay_triangulation_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <DT2> CGAL::Delaunay_triangulation_caching_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <DT2> CGAL::Regular_triangulation_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <RT2> CGAL::Regular_triangulation_caching_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <RT2> CGAL::Segment_Delaunay_graph_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <SDG2> CGAL::Segment_Delaunay_graph_caching_degeneracy_removal_policy_2 <SDG2> Draw Voronoi Diagram CGAL::draw<VD>() GraphicsSceneOptionsVoronoiDiagram2 CGAL::Graphics_scene_options_voronoi_2

members.name: draw
members.brief: opens a new window and draws a 2D voronoi diagram.
members.detailed: Parameters of the drawing are taken from the optional graphics scene options parameter. A call to this function blocks the execution of the program until the drawing window is closed. This function requires CGAL_Qt6 , and is only available if the macro CGAL_USE_BASIC_VIEWER is defined. Linking with the cmake target CGAL::CGAL_Basic_viewer will link with CGAL_Qt6 and add the definition CGAL_USE_BASIC_VIEWER . VD2 which must be an instantiation of a CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2 <...> . GSOptions a model of GraphicsSceneOptionsVoronoiDiagram2 concept. vd2 the voronoi diagram to draw. gso the graphics scene options parameter. Advanced The real declaration of this function template is: template<class DG, class AT, class AP, class GSOptions> void CGAL::draw(const CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2<DG, AT, AP>& vd2, const GSOptions& gso);

members.name: draw
members.brief: A shortcut to CGAL::draw (vd2, Graphics_scene_options_voronoi_diagram_2 {}) .

members.name: add_to_graphics_scene
members.brief: adds the vertices, edges and faces of vd2 into the given graphic scene gs .
members.detailed: Parameters of the cells are taken from the optional graphics scene options parameter gso . Note that gs is not cleared before being filled (to enable to draw several data structures in the same basic viewer). VD2 which must be an instantiation of a CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2 <...> . GSOptions a model of GraphicsSceneOptionsVoronoiDiagram2 concept. vd2 the voronoi diagram to draw. gs the graphic scene to fill. gso the graphics scene options parameter. Advanced The real declaration of this function template is: template<class DG, class AT, class AP, class GSOptions> void CGAL::add_to_graphics_scene(const CGAL::Voronoi_diagram_2<DG, AT, AP>& vd2, CGAL::Graphics_scene& gs, const GSOptions& gso);

members.name: add_to_graphics_scene
members.brief: A shortcut to CGAL::add_to_graphics_scene (vd2, gs, Graphics_scene_options_voronoi_diagram_2 {}) .
